results.description: The login system at https://example.com/login lacks rate limiting, account lockout, and multi-factor authentication. Brute force attacks are feasible.
results.recommendation: Implement rate limiting, account lockout after failed attempts, and multi-factor authentication. Use strong password policies.

results.description: SQL injection vulnerability detected in the login form at https://example.com/login. The 'username' parameter is directly concatenated into SQL queries without parameterization.
results.recommendation: Use parameterized queries / prepared statements. Implement input validation and use an ORM framework.

results.description: Forms at https://example.com lack CSRF tokens. An attacker can craft malicious requests that execute authenticated actions without user consent.
results.recommendation: Implement anti-CSRF tokens for all state-changing operations. Use SameSite cookie attribute and validate Origin headers.

results.description: Reflected XSS vulnerability found in the search functionality at https://example.com/search. User input in the 'q' parameter is rendered without sanitization.
results.recommendation: Implement output encoding using Content Security Policy (CSP). Sanitize all user inputs with libraries like DOMPurify.

results.description: IDOR vulnerability in API endpoint https://example.com/api/users. User IDs are sequential and accessible without proper authorization checks.
results.recommendation: Implement proper access control checks. Use UUID-based identifiers and validate user ownership of resources.

results.description: The application at https://example.com is missing critical security headers: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, and Content-Security-Policy.
results.recommendation: Add security headers: Strict-Transport-Security, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, and Referrer-Policy.

results.description: Directory listing is enabled on the server at https://example.com/backup, exposing sensitive files including configuration backups and database dumps.
results.recommendation: Disable directory listing on the web server. Configure appropriate directory permissions and use index files.

results.description: Server version information is exposed in HTTP response headers at https://example.com. This aids attackers in targeting known vulnerabilities.
results.recommendation: Disable server banner in response headers. Configure web server to hide version details.
